data.title: Endeavour Mining (TSX:EDV) Return To Profitability Tests Bullish Growth Narratives
data.description:
Endeavour Mining (TSX:EDV) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$1.27 billion and basic EPS of US$0.28, rounding out a trailing twelve month picture of US$4.23 billion in revenue and EPS of US$2.80 as the business stays firmly in profitable territory. Over the past year, revenue has moved from US$2.68 billion in the 2024 fourth quarter TTM period and EPS of a US$1.20 loss to the current TTM figures, while quarterly revenue shifted from US$940.5 million and an EPS loss of US$0.49 in Q4 2024 to the latest US$1.27 billion and positive EPS print. With that backdrop, investors are likely to focus on how sustainably margins have reset after the return to profitability and what that might mean for the quality of future cash generation.

See our full analysis for Endeavour Mining.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the widely followed growth and risk narratives that have built around Endeavour Mining over the past year.

See what the community is saying about Endeavour Mining

Advertisement

TTM net income swings from loss to US$679.2 million

  • Over the trailing twelve months, net income excluding extra items is US$679.2 million, compared with a loss of US$293.9 million in the 2024 fourth quarter TTM period.
  • Bulls point to this shift to profitability as a key support for their case. However, the bullish narrative also assumes revenue will decline by 11.2% a year and earnings will move from US$492.2 million to US$269.2 million by 2029, so investors have to weigh:
    • How a profitable TTM profile of US$4.23b in revenue and US$2.80 in EPS fits with forecasts that build in lower future revenue and profit margins.
    • Whether paying a higher future P/E multiple, as the bullish scenario assumes, lines up with a business that analysts expect to be earning less absolute profit than the US$492.2 million referenced in the current TTM dataset.

Bulls argue that this earnings reset could support a very optimistic long term valuation, but the current TTM profit base and the assumption of shrinking revenue make it worth reading the full bull case before you decide how compelling it really is. 🐂 Endeavour Mining Bull Case

Quarterly EPS runs from US$1.12 peak to US$0.28 in Q4

  • Within FY 2025, basic EPS stepped through US$0.71 in Q1, US$1.12 in Q2 and US$0.69 in Q3 to US$0.28 in Q4, alongside quarterly net income excluding extra items moving from US$270.9 million in Q2 to US$67.9 million in Q4.
  • Bears focus on these intra year swings to argue that earnings may be fragile. Yet their own narrative assumes earnings rise from US$492.2 million to US$603.0 million by 2028 and profit margins increase from 12.6% to 18.1%, which raises questions such as:
    • How much weight to put on a single softer quarter like Q4 relative to a year that still produced US$679.2 million of TTM net income.
    • Whether the higher margin path that cautious analysts build into their models aligns with concerns about cost inflation and royalty changes that could work in the opposite direction.

Skeptics warn that swings like Q2 to Q4 could repeat, but the higher margin path in their own numbers means the detailed bear case is worth reading closely. 🐻 Endeavour Mining Bear Case

P/E of 21.8x sits below peers but above industry

  • At a share price of CA$83.30, the stock is cited as trading on a 21.8x P/E, below the 25.8x peer average yet above the 19.1x Canadian metals and mining industry P/E, alongside a stated DCF fair value of CA$136.70 and an analyst price target of CA$95.39.
  • The consensus narrative highlights this combination of forecast earnings growth of about 28.1% a year and revenue growth of 10.2% a year together with an assessment that the shares sit below DCF fair value. This invites you to think through:
    • How a current year where TTM revenue is US$4.23b and TTM EPS is US$2.80 frames expectations for that future growth path.
    • Whether an unstable dividend record and past five year earnings declines of 7.3% a year sit comfortably alongside forecasts that point to stronger earnings and revenue growth than the wider Canadian market.

data.title: Aquestive Therapeutics (AQST) Q4 Loss Surge Tests Bullish Margin Expansion Narrative
data.description:
Aquestive Therapeutics (AQST) just wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 total revenue of US$13.0 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.26, alongside a trailing twelve month EPS loss of US$0.78 on US$44.5 million of revenue. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$8.7 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.24 in Q1 2025 to US$10.0 million with a US$0.14 EPS loss in Q2, US$12.8 million with a US$0.14 EPS loss in Q3, and then US$13.0 million with a US$0.26 EPS loss in Q4. This leaves investors weighing top line momentum against pressure on margins and the pace of loss reduction.

See our full analysis for Aquestive Therapeutics.

With the latest report on the table, the next step is to see how these numbers stack up against the prevailing stories around Aquestive Therapeutics and which of those narratives hold up under the data.

See what the community is saying about Aquestive Therapeutics

Advertisement

US$44.5m in TTM revenue versus US$83.8m in losses

  • Over the trailing twelve months, Aquestive booked US$44.5 million of revenue alongside a net loss of US$83.8 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.78, so every dollar of sales is still matched by almost US$2 of loss.
  • Bulls point to the potential for Anaphylm and the broader PharmFilm portfolio to drive higher operating leverage over time, yet the current loss profile means that
    • the bullish scenario of margins rising from about a 147.4% loss to a 4.1% profit by around 2028 starts from a base where FY 2025 net income remains deeply negative at US$31.9 million in Q4 alone, and
    • the idea of long term margin expansion has to be weighed against the trailing cash burn implied by US$83.8 million of losses on a relatively modest revenue base.

Bulls argue that recent revenue traction could be the early step toward the kind of earnings swing their forecasts assume, and that is exactly the tension the detailed bull case tries to unpack in more depth. 🐂 Aquestive Therapeutics Bull Case

Premium 11.7x P/S with negative equity

  • Shares change hands at an 11.7x P/S ratio, compared with 4.3x for the US pharmaceuticals industry and 6.5x for peers, while the company also reports negative shareholders' equity over the trailing twelve months.
  • Bears highlight that paying a premium multiple for a business that is still loss making and carrying negative equity leaves little room for setbacks, especially when
    • the company is forecast to remain unprofitable for at least the next three years despite revenue being forecast to grow at about 30.4% per year, and
    • recent shareholder dilution and a highly volatile share price add extra financing and market risk on top of that rich 11.7x P/S starting point.

Skeptics warn that when a US$4.26 stock already prices in a premium P/S and balance sheet pressure, any wobble in execution can matter more than usual, which is exactly what the detailed bear case lays out numerically. 🐻 Aquestive Therapeutics Bear Case

Quarterly losses widened again in Q4 2025

  • Q4 2025 net loss of US$31.9 million compares with a US$15.4 million loss in Q3 2025 and US$17.1 million in Q4 2024, even though quarterly revenue in Q4 2025 was US$13.0 million versus US$12.8 million in Q3 2025 and US$11.9 million in Q4 2024.
